author: Ganga Dhar
title: Qabahat-us-Shafaat
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to show certain defects in the doctrine of 'Intercession of Muhammad' as believed in Islam.
notes: A Hindu-authored attack on the Islamic doctrine of the Prophet's intercession (shafa'at) — the Arya-Samaj vs Islam polemic front again (cf. p40 s5); reg 372 is late in the year's sequence. The anti-Islam critique cluster.

author: Goldsake, Reverend W.
title: Yanabi-ul-Quran
gloss: Or the sources of the Quran. A book in which the author has tried to show that the Quran is not of divine origin. Translated from the original in English and published by the Christian Literature Society for India, Punjab Branch.
publisher: Christian Literature Society for India, Punjab Branch
notes: 'The Sources of the Quran' — a Christian-missionary (Rev. W. Goldsack) attack on the Quran's divine origin, translated into Urdu; the classic orientalist-missionary polemic (Goldsack's well-known series) entering the Punjab controversy market. Reg 5 = very early registration. The Christian-vs-Islam front (complementing the Hindu-vs-Islam and intra-Muslim fronts on these pages — the full polemical ecology).

title: Hanuman Chalisa
gloss: Or a prayer addressed to the Hindu god Hanuman, which is repeated for forty days in seclusion or in a temple.
notes: The Hanuman Chalisa (Tulsidas's 40-verse Hanuman hymn) in Urdu script — canonical Hindu devotion in the Urdu-print medium; the everyday devotional core amid all the polemic. Its presence in an 'Urdu—Religion' section shows Hindu scripture circulating in Perso-Arabic script.

title: Khuda-i-Islam
gloss: A booklet criticising the conception of God in Islam.
notes: 'The God of Islam' — another Christian Literature Society critique of the Islamic conception of God (reg 4, the year's earliest cluster); the missionary polemical program (cf. s8). The Christian-missionary anti-Islam front is well represented.

author: Mir Muhammad, Murid
title: Tauhid-i-Rahmani
gloss: A book showing from the Islamic point of view the blessings of good deeds and the consequences of bad deeds, with quotations from the Quran and traditions.
notes: Ethical-theological treatise on reward and punishment (Quran + hadith) — the constructive/edificatory (as opposed to polemical) Islamic religious writing.

author: Muhammad Bashir, M.
title: Al-Qaul-ul-Mahmud
gloss: A pamphlet written in refutation of the decrees given by Maulvis Nazir Ahmad of Delhi and Abdus Samad of Bulandshahr, permitting the taking of interest by Muhammadans.
notes: An intra-Sunni fatwa-war over riba (interest): refuting named ulama who had permitted interest-taking — the fiqh debate on Islamic finance/usury made public in cheap print, 2000 copies free. Documents the modern-economy-vs-shariah tension and the fragmentation of ulama authority. Dual date 1327 Hijri.

author: Muhammad Hasan, Hakim
title: Fazail-i-Murtazawi
gloss: A book describing the miracles performed by Ali, the Fourth Caliph of Islam.
notes: Miracles of Ali (fazail = virtues/merits) — more Ali-centred Shia devotion from the Yusufi Press (cf. p24, p35 s28, p40 s6); the press's consistent Shia-hagiographic program across the quarter. Dual date 1326 Hijri.

author: Muhammad Zafar
title: Rafiq-ul-Atfal
gloss: A pamphlet on precepts relating to the performance of daily prayers for Muhammadan children.
notes: 'Children's Companion' — a children's prayer/catechism primer, 3000 copies free, from an anjuman (Muslim association); the mass religious-education-of-children effort. Marked '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.
